title: Verify next-lap PitStop shift as a transductive label identity
summary: The target is PitNextLap and the test rows still include PitStop.
rationale: The target is PitNextLap and the test rows still include PitStop. If rows form complete driver-race lap sequences, then PitNextLap may be nearly equal to the next row's PitStop after sorting by Race_Year/Race, Driver, LapNumber, and id. This is a validation trap worth testing before adding more weak tyre features.
implementation_hint: Create NextObservedPitStop = lead(PitStop) within each driver-race sequence on train+test separately; on train, measure AUC/agreement against PitNextLap. If agreement is very high, use the shifted value directly or blend it with the current model for ambiguous final-lap/duplicate-lap rows.
expected_effect: Potentially very large public score jump if the dataset exposes future covariates; otherwise quickly falsified and useful as a leakage audit.
risk: May fail if PitStop is not current-lap stop, sequences are incomplete, or Kaggle private rows break the batch-transductive assumption.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
